what_it_does: A grounding-confirmation result record for the 'A2-canvas-desktop.html' mockup (the 'Running Chain' canvas workspace). It lists 31 candidate UI-element dossiers clustered into 17 clusters, then sorts representative dossiers into 'confirmed' (7) and 'flagged' (11) by a multi-layer verdict pipeline (refcheck floor + a 3-draw accuracy jury). Each dossier carries an addressable ui:// id, capabilities, grounding state, a how-to/what/what-you-can-do triple, a maps_to_feature inventory id, and a represents label.
observations: Top-level 'ground' block gives a plain-language 'what_this_is' / 'what_you_can_do' summary of the whole screen.
Counts: n_candidates=31, n_dossiers=31, n_clusters=17; clustering reduces candidates into representative dossiers with cluster_members index lists.
Two verdict layers are named in 'reasons': Layer 1 'refcheck (deterministic floor)' and Layer 2 'accuracy jury (SOFT — variance not error)' with a 3-draw grounded/ungrounded vote.
refcheck flags 'feature fabrication' when maps_to_feature is neither an inventory id nor 'proposed' (e.g. RUN-force, CAN-modes).
ui://canvas/tabbar passed the jury unanimously (3/0) yet is FLAGGED solely on the refcheck floor for fabricated feature 'CAN-modes' — shows the deterministic floor can override a passing jury.
Each dossier distinguishes grounding 'built' (points to real code regions like Toolbar.tsx, Footer.tsx, NodeShape.tsx) vs 'proposed' (mockup-only, no code path).
Addresses use a ui:// scheme with region paths like ui://toolbar/presence, ui://canvas/memo-gate.
standouts: The SOFT-jury framing: 'variance not error' — non-quorum is treated as something for operator scrutiny rather than a failure.
A unanimous jury pass can still be FLAGGED by the deterministic refcheck floor (tabbar/CAN-modes case).
memo-gate and force recur as the conceptual spine — caching/skip-on-unchanged-hash and force-past-memo.
conventions: Follows the dossier schema: address, capabilities[], grounding, howto{how_to_change,what,what_you_can_do}, maps_to_feature, represents.
Capability vocabulary is consistent across files: pointable, spotlit, presentable, openable, driven.
Feature ids namespaced by domain prefix (RUN-, CAN-, NODE-, INB-, WIRE-, ENG-).
Every confirmed/flagged entry repeats the parent 'mockup' filename.
notes: This is one mockup's element-grounding ledger: which addressable UI dossiers passed the floor+jury (confirmed) vs need operator eyes (flagged), with code-region pointers for the built ones.
questions: What inventory defines the legal maps_to_feature ids that refcheck validates against?
Why is the SOFT jury quorum threshold set where a 1-of-3 grounded vote fails but a 2-of-3 passes?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a confirmation-batch output and its contents are exactly clustered dossiers with confirm verdicts.
apparent_themes: Addressable UI elements (ui:// scheme) as first-class grounded objects
Multi-layer verification: deterministic floor + probabilistic jury
intention_signals: Trying to verify, element by element, that each mockup affordance is truthfully grounded in real code/features (or honestly marked proposed), and to surface anything uncertain for human review rather than silently asserting it.
novelty: The dual-gate verdict where a hard refcheck floor can veto a unanimously-passed soft jury; the 'variance not error' framing of jury disagreement.
contribution: Contributes the per-element grounding verdicts and code-region pointers for the canvas-desktop 'Running Chain' surface, plus the dual-layer confirm schema (refcheck floor + soft jury) that a fused system would keep for adjudicating UI-element groundedness.
summary_for_embedding: Grounding-confirmation batch for the A2 canvas-desktop 'Running Chain' mockup: 31 candidate UI dossiers clustered to 17, sorted into confirmed vs flagged by a refcheck floor plus a 3-draw accuracy jury.
intention_for_embedding: To verify that each addressable UI element of the canvas-desktop mockup is honestly grounded in real code/features and to flag uncertain ones for operator review.
